checks.1_host_id: WARN: HOST_ID unset — cannot SSH-probe. Pod assumed to be local.

preflight_dry_run_result.interpretation: Local dev HF_TOKEN FAIL is expected. Pod-side preflight (run via bash runner inside launch_alm_a1.hexa) re-checks HF_TOKEN + nvidia-smi + disk, and refuses to launch if any fail. The blocker 'hf_token' is pod-operator-owned, NOT a code defect.

non_blocking_for_code: Local preflight fails on HF_TOKEN — expected; pod-side re-check will gate.
Local preflight SKIPs nvidia-smi check — by design; pod-side gate catches this.
operator_responsibilities_on_pod: export HF_TOKEN=<...> (from ~/.runpod/secrets or infra secret bag)
ensure /workspace/anima is up-to-date (git pull) so launch_alm_a1.hexa + ckpt_backup_hook.hexa are both current
ensure scripts/ckpt_backup_hook.hexa is staged (E1/E2/E3 agent will merge via agent worktree)

ready_for_launch_rationale: Launcher + config + preflight + runbook + smoke test all pass static + dry-run validation. Pod-side gate in the emitted bash runner re-checks HF_TOKEN / GPU / disk / R2 prefix at launch time. Operator has a single command ready: `hexa run training/launch_alm_a1.hexa` on the pod (inside tmux). Per user constraint, this task stops at ready-to-launch state — actual invocation is operator's choice.
